titre: Anglais A1 — Module 1 · Se présenter (Pistes 1–10)

pages.title: Introduction
pages.content:
Module 1 — Se présenter en anglais

Dans ce module, vous allez apprendre à :

  • Dire bonjour et au revoir en anglais
  • Vous présenter : nom, prénom, âge, nationalité
  • Poser des questions simples sur quelqu'un
  • Utiliser les verbes to be et to have au présent

Mots-clés : Hello · Hi · My name is · I am · How are you? · Nice to meet you

pages.title: Dialogue — Nice to meet you!
pages.transcript:
🎧 Écoutez le dialogue (Piste 1)

Emma: Hi! My name is Emma. What's your name?

Lucas: Hello! I'm Lucas. Nice to meet you, Emma.

Emma: Nice to meet you too! Where are you from?

Lucas: I'm from France. And you?

Emma: I'm from England. How old are you?

Lucas: I'm sixteen. And you?

Emma: I'm fifteen.

Traduction

Emma : Salut ! Je m'appelle Emma. Comment tu t'appelles ?

Lucas : Bonjour ! Je suis Lucas. Enchanté, Emma.

Emma : Enchantée aussi ! Tu viens d'où ?

Lucas : Je viens de France. Et toi ?

Emma : Je viens d'Angleterre. Tu as quel âge ?

Lucas : J'ai seize ans. Et toi ?

Emma : J'ai quinze ans.

pages.title: Dialogue — At school
pages.transcript:
🎧 Écoutez le dialogue (Piste 2)

Teacher: Good morning, class! My name is Mr. Brown. I'm your English teacher.

Student: Good morning, Mr. Brown!

Teacher: What is your name?

Student: My name is Sofia. I'm from Morocco.

Teacher: Welcome, Sofia! Are you a new student?

Student: Yes, I am. I'm in Year 10.

Traduction

Professeur : Bonjour, la classe ! Je m'appelle M. Brown. Je suis votre professeur d'anglais.

Élève : Bonjour, M. Brown !

Professeur : Comment vous appelez-vous ?

Élève : Je m'appelle Sofia. Je viens du Maroc.

Professeur : Bienvenue, Sofia ! Vous êtes un nouvel élève ?

Élève : Oui. Je suis en 4ème.

pages.title: Grammaire — Le verbe TO BE
pages.content:
📖 Le verbe to be (être) au présent

PronomForme complèteForme contractéeTraduction
II amI'mJe suis
YouYou areYou'reTu es / Vous êtes
HeHe isHe'sIl est
SheShe isShe'sElle est
ItIt isIt'sC'est (neutre)
WeWe areWe'reNous sommes
TheyThey areThey'reIls/Elles sont

Forme négative

Ajoutez not après le verbe :

  • I am not French. → I'm not French.
  • He is not a student. → He isn't a student.

Forme interrogative

Inversez le sujet et le verbe :

  • Are you French? — Yes, I am. / No, I'm not.
  • Is she a teacher? — Yes, she is. / No, she isn't.

pages.title: Exercices — Module 1
pages.content:
✏️ Exercices

Exercice 1 — Complétez avec la bonne forme de to be

  1. I ___ fifteen years old. (am / is / are)
  2. She ___ from England. (am / is / are)
  3. We ___ students. (am / is / are)
  4. They ___ not French. (am / is / are)
  5. ___ you a new student? (Am / Is / Are)

Exercice 2 — Traduisez en anglais

  1. Je m'appelle Karim.
  2. J'ai quatorze ans.
  3. Je viens du Maroc.
  4. Enchanté de vous rencontrer.
  5. Elle n'est pas professeur.

Exercice 3 — Posez la question

Exemple : She is French. → Is she French?

  1. He is a student.
  2. They are from England.
  3. You are sixteen.

pages.title: Bilan — Module 1
pages.content:
✅ Bilan du Module 1

Ce que vous avez appris :

  • ✅ Les salutations : Hello, Good morning, Goodbye…
  • ✅ Se présenter : My name is… / I'm from… / I'm … years old

  • ✅ Vocabulaire de base : les nationalités, les salutations

Auto-évaluation

Pouvez-vous :

  • ☐ Vous présenter en 3 phrases ?
  • ☐ Demander et donner l'âge de quelqu'un ?
  • ☐ Conjuguer to be à toutes les personnes ?

Prochain module : La famille et le domicile 🏠

titre: Anglais A1 — Module 2 · La famille et la maison (Pistes 11–20)

pages.title: Introduction
pages.content:
Module 2 — La famille et la maison

Dans ce module, vous allez apprendre à :

  • Parler de votre famille en anglais
  • Décrire les membres de votre famille
  • Présenter votre maison et les pièces
  • Utiliser les adjectifs possessifs : my, your, his, her…

Mots-clés : family · mother · father · brother · sister · house · bedroom · kitchen

pages.title: Dialogue — My family
pages.transcript:
🎧 Écoutez le dialogue (Piste 11)

Tom: Look! This is a photo of my family.

Sara: Oh nice! Who is this?

Tom: This is my mother. Her name is Mary. And this is my father, John.

Sara: Do you have brothers or sisters?

Tom: Yes! I have one brother and two sisters. My brother is twelve and my sisters are eight and ten.

Sara: What a big family!

Traduction

Tom : Regarde ! C'est une photo de ma famille.

Sara : Oh sympa ! C'est qui ça ?

Tom : C'est ma mère. Elle s'appelle Mary. Et voici mon père, John.

Sara : Tu as des frères ou des sœurs ?

Tom : Oui ! J'ai un frère et deux sœurs. Mon frère a douze ans et mes sœurs ont huit et dix ans.

Sara : Quelle grande famille !

pages.content:
📖 Les adjectifs possessifs

Exercice 1 — Complétez avec l'adjectif possessif

  2. Tom has a dog. ___ dog is small.

Relisez le dialogue et répondez :

  2. Tom has two brothers. (True / False)

Prochain module : La vie quotidienne ⏰

titre: Anglais A1 — Module 3 · La vie quotidienne (Pistes 21–30)

pages.title: Introduction
pages.content:
Module 3 — La vie quotidienne

  • Décrire votre routine quotidienne
  • Dire l'heure en anglais
  • Utiliser le présent simple (affirmation, négation, question)
  • Fréquences : always, often, sometimes, never

Mots-clés : wake up · have breakfast · go to school · study · have lunch · play · sleep

pages.title: Dialogue — A typical day
pages.transcript:
🎧 Piste 21

Maya: What time do you wake up?

Jack: I wake up at seven o'clock. Then I have breakfast at half past seven.

Maya: Do you walk to school?

Jack: No, I don't. I take the bus. School starts at eight thirty.

Maya: What do you do after school?

Jack: I usually play football with my friends. Then I do my homework and have dinner with my family.

Traduction

Maya : À quelle heure tu te lèves ? Jack : Je me lève à sept heures. Puis je prends le petit-déjeuner à sept heures et demie. Maya : Tu vas à l'école à pied ? Jack : Non. Je prends le bus. L'école commence à huit heures trente. Maya : Qu'est-ce que tu fais après l'école ? Jack : Je joue généralement au football avec mes amis. Puis je fais mes devoirs et dîne avec ma famille.

pages.title: Dialogue — At the market
pages.transcript:
🎧 Piste 31

Customer: Good morning! Can I have some apples, please?

Vendor: Of course! How many would you like?

Customer: Six apples, please. And do you have any oranges?

Vendor: Yes, we do. They're very fresh today!

Customer: Great! I'll take a kilo. How much is that?

Vendor: That's two pounds fifty, please.

Traduction

Client : Bonjour ! Je peux avoir des pommes, s'il vous plaît ? Vendeur : Bien sûr ! Combien en voulez-vous ? Client : Six pommes, s'il vous plaît. Et vous avez des oranges ? Vendeur : Oui. Elles sont très fraîches aujourd'hui ! Client : Super ! J'en prendrai un kilo. C'est combien ? Vendeur : C'est deux livres cinquante, s'il vous plaît.

pages.title: Grammaire — Some / Any
pages.content:
📖 Some / Any — Dénombrable / Indénombrable

Dénombrable (countable) = on peut compter : an apple, two apples

Indénombrable (uncountable) = on ne peut pas compter : milk, bread, water

SomeAny
AffirmatifI have some apples.—
Négatif—I don't have any milk.
QuestionWould you like some tea?Do you have any eggs?

How many + dénombrable : How many apples?

How much + indénombrable : How much milk?

pages.title: Exercices — Module 4
pages.content:
✏️ Exercices

Some ou Any ?

  1. I'd like ___ orange juice.
  2. Do you have ___ bread?
  3. There isn't ___ milk in the fridge.
  4. Can I have ___ apples?

How much ou How many ?

  1. ___ eggs do you need?
  2. ___ sugar is in your coffee?
  3. ___ bananas can I eat?

titre: Anglais A1 — Module 5 · Loisirs et sports (Pistes 41–50)

pages.title: Introduction
pages.content:
Module 5 — Loisirs et sports

  • Nommer les loisirs et sports courants
  • Exprimer vos goûts : like / love / hate / don't like
  • Le présent continu : I am playing…
  • Différence présent simple vs présent continu

pages.title: Dialogue — What are you doing?
pages.transcript:
🎧 Piste 41

Lena: Hi! What are you doing?

Max: I'm watching a football match on TV. My favourite team is playing!

Lena: Do you like football?

Max: I love it! I play football every Saturday. Do you play sports?

Lena: Yes, I like swimming and cycling. But I don't like team sports very much.

Max: That's okay! Sport is important for health.

Traduction

Lena : Salut ! Qu'est-ce que tu fais ? Max : Je regarde un match de foot à la télé. Mon équipe préférée joue ! Lena : Tu aimes le foot ? Max : Je l'adore ! Je joue au foot tous les samedis. Tu fais du sport ? Lena : Oui, j'aime la natation et le cyclisme. Mais je n'aime pas trop les sports d'équipe. Max : C'est bien ! Le sport est important pour la santé.

pages.title: Grammaire — Présent continu
pages.content:
📖 Le présent continu (be + -ing)

Utilisé pour une action en cours au moment où on parle.

FormeExemple
AffirmativeI am playing football.
NégativeShe is not watching TV.
InterrogativeAre you reading?

Présent simple vs Présent continu

  • I play football every Saturday. (habitude)
  • I am playing football right now. (maintenant)

Module 6 — La ville et les transports

pages.title: Dialogue — Excuse me, where is…?
pages.transcript:
🎧 Piste 51

Tourist: Excuse me! Where is the nearest bus stop?

Local: It's not far. Go straight ahead, then turn left at the traffic lights.

Tourist: Turn left at the traffic lights. OK.

Local: Yes. The bus stop is next to the supermarket, opposite the park.

Tourist: How far is it?

Local: About five minutes on foot.

Tourist: Thank you so much!

Traduction

Touriste : Excusez-moi ! Où est l'arrêt de bus le plus proche ? Local : Ce n'est pas loin. Allez tout droit, puis tournez à gauche aux feux. Touriste : Tourner à gauche aux feux. D'accord. Local : Oui. L'arrêt de bus est à côté du supermarché, en face du parc. Touriste : C'est à quelle distance ? Local : Environ cinq minutes à pied. Touriste : Merci beaucoup !

pages.title: Grammaire — Prépositions et impératif

📖 Prépositions de lieu

✏️ Révision générale

1. To be — Complétez

  1. She ___ a student from Morocco.
  2. We ___ not at home today.
  3. ___ they your friends?

2. Présent simple

  1. He ___ (play) football every day.
  2. I ___ (not / eat) meat.
  3. ___ she ___ (speak) English?

  2. She ___ (not / sleep) — she ___ (read).

titre: Expressing Time in English — Grammar & Exercises

pages.title: Introduction — Expressing Time
pages.content:
⏰ English Grammar

Expressing Time in English

Learn to talk about time using the right preposition every time.

🎯 Lesson Objectives
  • Use AT, IN, ON correctly for times, months, and days
  • Distinguish FOR (duration) from SINCE (starting point)
  • Use DURING, BEFORE, AFTER, BY, AGO accurately
  • Avoid the most common time preposition mistakes

In this lesson: at · in · on · for · since · during · before · after · by · ago

pages.title: AT — IN — ON
pages.content:
📖 The three main time prepositions

PrepositionWhen to use itExamples
AT Exact time · moment of day · public holidays at 8 o'clock · at noon · at midnight · at Christmas
= à 8h · à midi · à minuit · à Noël
IN Month · season · year · century · part of day in July · in summer · in 2020 · in the morning
= en juillet · en été · en 2020 · le matin
ON Day of the week · specific date on Monday · on 1st July · on Monday morning
= lundi · le 1er juillet · lundi matin
⚠️ No preposition with: last / next / this / every
last week · next Monday · this morning · every day

pages.title: FOR — SINCE — DURING
pages.content:
📖 Talking about duration and periods

PrepositionUsageExamples
FOR Duration — how long I studied for two hours.
She lived here for 5 years.
= pendant deux heures / pendant 5 ans
SINCE Starting point — since when I have lived here since 2015.
It has been raining since Monday.
= depuis 2015 / depuis lundi
DURING Throughout a period During the summer · during the meeting
= pendant l'été / pendant la réunion
💡 FOR answers "How long?" → for 3 years
💡 SINCE answers "Since when?" → since 2020

pages.title: BEFORE — AFTER — BY — AGO
pages.content:
📖 Placing events in time

WordMeaningExample
BEFORE earlier than Finish before Friday. / Before going out, check the weather.
= avant vendredi / avant de sortir
AFTER later than After lunch, we went for a walk.
= après le déjeuner
BY no later than (deadline) I'll be there by 6 pm. / Submit it by Monday.
= au plus tard à 18h / d'ici lundi
AGO in the past (from now) I arrived two days ago. / He left an hour ago.
= il y a deux jours / il y a une heure

pages.content:

Choose: AT · IN · ON

  1. I was born ___ 15th August.

  4. We go skiing ___ winter.

FOR or SINCE?

  1. I have known her ___ ten years.
  2. He has been sick ___ Monday.

Translate these sentences

pages.title: Quick Reference — Time Prepositions
pages.content:
📋 Quick Reference Card

AT  at 8h · at noon · at night · at Christmas · at the weekend

IN  in July · in 2024 · in summer · in the morning · in the 20th century

ON  on Monday · on 1st July · on Monday morning

FOR  for 2 hours / years / days (duration)

SINCE  since Monday / 2015 / yesterday (starting point)

DURING  during the film / meeting / holidays

BY  by Friday = no later than Friday

AGO  2 days ago = il y a 2 jours

pages.title: Dialogue — Making Plans
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — Making Plans

Listen and read. Notice how time prepositions are used naturally.

Emma  Are you free on Saturday?

Tom  Yes! What time?

Emma  Let's meet at 3 o'clock in the afternoon.

Tom  Perfect. I've been waiting for weeks to see that film!

Emma  Me too. I saw the trailer two days ago and it looks amazing.

Tom  Great. Before the film, shall we get coffee?

Emma  Sure — by 2:30 at the café?

Tom  Deal. See you then!

🔍 Spot the prepositions: on Saturday · at 3 o'clock · in the afternoon · for weeks · two days ago · before the film · by 2:30

pages.title: Summary — What You Learned
pages.content:
🏁 Summary — Expressing Time in English

AT  exact time / moment of day: at 8h · at noon · at Christmas

IN  month / season / year: in July · in summer · in 2020

ON  day / date: on Monday · on 1st July

FOR  duration: for 2 hours  |  SINCE  starting point: since 2015

DURING  throughout a period: during the film

BY  deadline: by Friday  |  AGO  past: 2 days ago

✅ Self-check — Can you…
  • Say what time you do something each day? (AT / IN / ON)
  • Say how long you have done something? (FOR / SINCE)
  • Talk about a past event? (AGO / BEFORE / AFTER)
  • Give a deadline? (BY)

titre: Expressing a Place in English — Grammar & Exercises

pages.title: Introduction — Expressing a Place
pages.content:
📍 English Grammar

Expressing a Place in English

Place prepositions describe where someone or something is.

🎯 Lesson Objectives
  • Use IN, AT, ON correctly for locations
  • Use UNDER, ABOVE, OVER, BELOW for vertical position
  • Use NEXT TO, BETWEEN, OPPOSITE, BEHIND, IN FRONT OF
  • Describe the position of objects and people in a scene

In this lesson: in · at · on · under · above · in front of · behind · next to · between · around · opposite · far from

pages.title: IN — AT — ON (place)
pages.content:
📖 The three core place prepositions

PrepositionWhen to use itExamples
IN Inside an enclosed space · inside a city or country in the room · in London · in Morocco · in the car
= dans la pièce · à Londres · au Maroc · dans la voiture
AT Specific point · functional place at the door · at school · at work · at home · at the station
= à la porte · à l'école · au travail · à la maison
ON On a surface · on a floor · on a street on the table · on the wall · on the floor · on the road
= sur la table · sur le mur · sur le sol · sur la route

pages.title: UNDER — ABOVE — OVER — BELOW
pages.content:
📖 Vertical position

WordMeaningExample
under below, beneath The cat is under the table.
= sous la table
above higher than (no contact) The lamp is above the desk.
= au-dessus du bureau
over above + covering / movement Jump over the fence. / A bridge over the river.
= par-dessus la clôture
below lower than (level) The temperature is below zero.
= en dessous de zéro

pages.title: NEXT TO — BETWEEN — OPPOSITE
pages.content:
📖 Relative position

WordMeaningExample
next to / beside at the side of The bank is next to the pharmacy.
= à côté de la pharmacie
in front of facing, before There's a garden in front of the house.
= devant la maison
behind at the back of The car is parked behind the building.
= derrière le bâtiment
between in the middle of two things The library is between the school and the park.
= entre l'école et le parc
opposite facing, across from The hotel is opposite the station.
= en face de la gare
around surrounding, on all sides There are trees around the lake.
= autour du lac

pages.title: Practice Exercises
pages.content:
✏️ Practice

Choose: IN · AT · ON

  1. She lives ___ Paris.
  2. The book is ___ the shelf.
  3. He's ___ work right now.
  4. The map is ___ the wall.
  5. We met ___ the airport.
  6. I put the keys ___ my bag.

Complete the sentences

  1. The supermarket is ___ the bank and the school. (between)
  2. The cat jumped ___ the wall. (over)
  3. The pharmacy is ___ the hospital. (opposite)
  4. There's a small café ___ the bookshop. (next to)

Describe your room

Write 5 sentences describing where objects are in your room. Use 5 different prepositions from this lesson.

pages.title: Quick Reference — Place Prepositions
pages.content:
📋 Quick Reference Card

IN  enclosed space — in the box / in London / in the car

AT  specific point — at school / at home / at the door

ON  surface — on the table / on the wall / on the floor

UNDER  below  |  ABOVE/OVER  above  |  BELOW  lower than

NEXT TO  beside  |  IN FRONT OF  facing  |  BEHIND  at the back

BETWEEN  middle of two  |  OPPOSITE  facing  |  AROUND  surrounding

pages.title: Dialogue — Finding the Library
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — Finding the Library

Read the conversation. Notice how place prepositions are used.

Sarah  Excuse me, is there a library near here?

Man  Yes! Go along this street, past the bank.

Sarah  Is it on the left or the right?

Man  It's between the post office and the school, opposite the park.

Sarah  Great. And is there a café in front of the library?

Man  Yes, there's one next to the entrance. You can't miss it!

Sarah  Perfect, thank you so much!

🔍 Spot the prepositions: near · on the left · between · opposite · in front of · next to

pages.title: Summary — What You Learned
pages.content:
🏁 Summary — Expressing Place in English

IN  enclosed space: in the room · in London · in the car

AT  specific point: at school · at home · at the station

ON  surface: on the table · on the wall · on the floor

UNDER  below  |  ABOVE  higher than  |  OVER  covering

NEXT TO  beside  |  IN FRONT OF  facing  |  BEHIND  at the back

BETWEEN  two things  |  OPPOSITE  facing  |  AROUND  surrounding

✅ Self-check — Can you…

  • Give directions using next to, opposite, behind, between?

titre: All English Prepositions (A1–A2)

pages.title: Introduction — All English Prepositions
pages.content:
🎓 English Grammar

All English Prepositions

A complete guide to English prepositions — from beginner to intermediate.

🎯 Lesson Objectives
  • Use prepositions of time: at, in, on, for, since, during, by, ago
  • Use prepositions of place: in, at, on, under, above, next to, between
  • Use prepositions of movement: to, from, into, out of, through, across
  • Use prepositions of manner & cause: by, with, because of, due to
  • Master fixed verb + preposition combinations

In this lesson: at · in · on · for · since · during · to · from · into · out of · through · across · by · with · because of

pages.title: Prepositions of Time
pages.content:
📖 Time Prepositions

PrepositionUsageExamples
ATExact time, moment of dayat 8 o'clock · at noon · at night · at Christmas
INMonth, season, year, centuryin July · in summer · in 2020 · in the morning
ONDay, dateon Monday · on 1st July · on Monday morning
FORDuration (how long)I studied for two hours.
SINCEStarting point (since when)I've lived here since 2015.
DURINGThroughout a periodDuring the summer · during the meeting
BYDeadline (no later than)Finish by Friday. · I'll be there by 6 pm.
AGOTime in the pastI arrived two days ago.
= Je suis arrivé il y a deux jours.
💡 FOR answers "How long?" — SINCE answers "Since when?"
I've been here for 3 years. / I've been here since 2021.

pages.title: Prepositions of Movement
pages.content:
📖 Movement & Direction

PrepositionMeaningExample
totowards / destinationGo to school. / Travel to London.
= Aller à l'école. / Voyager à Londres.
fromorigin / departure pointCome from Morocco. / A train from Paris.
= Venir du Maroc.
intoentering a spaceWalk into the room. / Jump into the pool.
out ofleaving a spaceGet out of the car. / Take it out of the bag.
throughpassing inside / acrossWalk through the forest. / Drive through the tunnel.
acrossfrom one side to the otherSwim across the river. / Walk across the street.
alongfollowing a line / pathWalk along the beach.
= Se promener le long de la plage.
pastgoing beyond / in front ofGo past the school, then turn left.

pages.title: Fixed Verb + Preposition
pages.content:
📖 Fixed Verb + Preposition Combinations

These combinations must be memorised — the preposition is fixed and cannot be changed.

Verb + PrepositionMeaningExample
listen toto listen to sthListen to the music.
look atto look at sth/sbLook at this photo.
wait forto wait for sb/sthWait for the bus.
talk aboutto talk about sthTalk about the problem.
think about/ofto think about sthThink about your future.
depend onto depend on sthIt depends on the weather.
agree withto agree with sbI agree with you.
look afterto take care of sb/sthShe looks after her brother.

pages.title: Practice Exercises
pages.content:
✏️ Practice

Choose the correct preposition

  1. She walked ___ the park every morning. (through / across)
  2. I travelled ___ bus from Casablanca. (by / with)
  3. The match was cancelled ___ rain. (because of / due to)
  4. Can you look ___ my bag while I'm gone? (at / after)
  5. She jumped ___ the pool. (into / in)
  6. I have been studying English ___ three years. (for / since)

  1. travel / by plane / to London

Correct the mistakes

  1. I was born in 15th August.
  2. She listened for the music all evening.
  3. We have known each other since five years.

pages.content:
📋 Quick Reference — All Prepositions

⏰ Time: at 8h · in July · on Monday · for 2 hours · since 2015 · during the film · by Friday · ago

📍 Place: in the room · at school · on the table · under · above · next to · between · opposite · behind · in front of

pages.title: Dialogue — Getting Around the City
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — Getting Around the City

Ali  How do you usually get to work?

Lena  I travel by bus. It goes through the city centre.

Ali  How long does it take?

Lena  About 30 minutes. I've been doing it since January.

Ali  Do you walk along the river from the bus stop?

Lena  Yes! I walk across the bridge and past the market. It's lovely!

Ali  That sounds great. I depend on my car — I should change!

🔍 Spot the prepositions: to work · by bus · through · since January · along · from · across · past · depend on

titre: A Letter from London (Reading & Writing)

pages.title: Introduction — A Letter from London
pages.content:
📖 Reading & Writing

A Letter from London

Read, understand, and write a personal letter in English.

🎯 Lesson Objectives
  • Read and understand an authentic English letter
  • Learn vocabulary related to London and city life
  • Understand formal and informal letter structure
  • Write your own personal letter in English (80–100 words)

In this lesson: reading comprehension · letter structure · London vocabulary · writing practice

pages.title: Text — A Letter from London
pages.content:
📖 Read the letter

15 Baker Street,
London, W1U 6RT
10th October 2024

Dear Yasmine,

How are you? I hope you and your family are doing well. I am writing to you from my new flat in London. I arrived here two weeks ago and I am slowly getting used to life in this amazing city.

London is a very big and busy city. The streets are full of people from all over the world. I love the red double-decker buses and the Underground — that's what they call the metro here! My flat is in the centre, near Hyde Park, which is a huge and beautiful park. I go there every morning for a walk.

The weather is quite cold and rainy, very different from home! But the city is incredible — there are museums, theatres, markets and parks everywhere. Last weekend, I visited the British Museum and Buckingham Palace. It was fantastic!

I miss you and our friends very much. I hope you can visit me soon. Please write back when you have time.

Best wishes,
Sophia

pages.content:

Letter Phrases

OpeningClosing

pages.content:

pages.title: Writing — Your Turn!
pages.content:
✏️ Write Your Own Letter!

Write a letter of 80–100 words to a friend about a city you live in or have visited. Use Sophia's letter as a model:

✅ Include:

  • How long have you been there?
  • Describe 2–3 things about the city
  • What have you done / seen?
  • What do you miss from home?
  • Use a proper closing phrase
💡 Start with: "Dear [Name], How are you? I am writing from..."

pages.title: Dialogue — Talking About the Letter
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — Talking About the Letter

Yasmine  I just got a letter from Sophia in London!

Nadia  Really? How is she? What does she say?

Yasmine  She's getting used to life there. Her flat is near Hyde Park.

Nadia  Incredible! Does she like London?

Yasmine  Yes! She loves the red double-decker buses and the Underground.

Nadia  What about the weather?

Yasmine  Cold and rainy! She misses us a lot. She wants us to visit soon.

Nadia  We should write back to her today!

pages.title: Text — My Gap Year Adventure
pages.content:
📖 Read the text

My Gap Year Adventure — by James

After finishing school, I decided to take a gap year and travel around the world. It was the best decision of my life!

My journey started in Morocco. I flew from London to Marrakech and spent two weeks exploring the medina, the souks, and the beautiful desert. The people were incredibly friendly and the food was delicious.

From Morocco, I took a ferry to Spain, then travelled by train through France and Italy. In Rome, I visited the Colosseum and ate the most amazing pasta. In Paris, I went up the Eiffel Tower at sunset — breathtaking!

My favourite part of the trip was Morocco. The colours, the smells, the music… everything was magical. I stayed in a traditional riad and rode a camel in the Sahara Desert!

Travelling taught me to be independent, open-minded, and grateful. I met people from every corner of the world and learned something new every single day. I would recommend a gap year to everyone!

pages.content:

James  Did you have a good holiday?

titre: Arabic A1 — Module 1 · The Arabic Alphabet (الحروف العربية)

pages.title: Introduction
pages.content:
Module 1 — The Arabic Alphabet (الحروف العربية)

Welcome to the A1 Arabic course! In this first module, you will:

  • Discover the 28 letters of the Arabic alphabet
  • Learn the writing direction: right to left (←)
  • Understand the initial, medial, final and isolated forms of each letter
  • Discover short vowels (harakat): fatha, kasra, damma

⚠️ Arabic is written and read from right to left. Take time to get used to it!

pages.title: Short Vowels — الحركات
pages.content:
📚 Short Vowels (الحركات)

In Arabic, short vowels are diacritical marks placed above or below letters:

SignNameSoundExample
َFatha/a/كَتَبَ
ِKasra/i/كِتَاب
ُDamma/u/كُتُب
ْSukun(no vowel)كَلْب

💡 In everyday texts, vowels are often not written. You learn them through practice.

pages.title: Grammar — Letter Forms
pages.content:
📖 The 4 Forms of Each Letter

In Arabic, each letter can take 4 forms depending on its position in the word:

LetterIsolatedInitialMedialFinal
Baa (ب)ببــبــب
Miim (م)ممــمــم
Nuun (ن)ننــنــن

⚠️ Some letters (like dal د and ra ر) only connect on the left — they have no initial/medial joined form.

pages.title: Exercises — Module 1
pages.content:
✏️ Exercises

Exercise 1 — Identify the letters

Write the name of each letter:

The Nominal Sentence (no verb "to be")

In Arabic, the verb "to be" is not used in the present tense!

pages.title: Grammar — Gender in Arabic
pages.content:
📖 Gender in Arabic (المذكر والمؤنث)

In Arabic, all nouns are either masculine (مذكر) or feminine (مؤنث).

The feminine is often formed by adding ة (ta marbuta) at the end:

MasculineFeminineMeaning
طَالِبطَالِبَةstudent (m/f)
مُدَرِّسمُدَرِّسَةteacher (m/f)
مَرِيضمَرِيضَةsick (m/f)

Possessive Suffixes

PersonSuffixExampleTranslation
My-يأَبِيmy father
Your (m)-كَأَبُوكَyour father (m)
His-هُأَبُوهُhis father
Her-هَاأَبُوهَاher father

pages.title: Grammar — Adjective Agreement
pages.content:
📖 Colour Adjective Agreement

In Arabic, the adjective follows the noun and agrees in gender:

كِتَاب أَحْمَر = a red book (masc.)

سَيَّارَة حَمْرَاء = a red car (fem.)

Saying your age:

عُمْرِي خَمْسَ عَشَرَة سَنَة = I am fifteen years old (lit. "My age is fifteen years")

كَمْ عُمْرُكَ؟ = How old are you? (masc.)

كَمْ عُمْرُكِ؟ = How old are you? (fem.)

pages.title: Dialogue — حوار يومي
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — حوار يومي (Daily Conversation)

Read the dialogue. Notice how time words are used.

سارة  ماذا فعلتَ أمس؟

أحمد  ذهبتُ إلى المكتبة في الصباح، ثم زرتُ صديقي بعد الظهر.

سارة  وماذا ستفعل غداً؟

أحمد  لديّ امتحان في الساعة التاسعة صباحاً.

سارة  هل أنت مستعد؟

أحمد  أدرس منذ أسبوع، دائماً في المساء.

سارة  حظ سعيد!

🔍 Time words used: أمس (yesterday) · في الصباح (in the morning) · بعد الظهر (in the afternoon) · غداً (tomorrow) · في الساعة (at … o'clock) · منذ (since/for) · دائماً (always)

في  in / on  |  منذ  since / for  |  خلال  during

قبل  before  |  بعد  after

pages.title: Quick Reference — ملخص
pages.content:
📋 Quick Reference — حروف الجر المكانية

في  in, inside — في الحقيبة · في المدينة

على  on — على الطاولة · على الجدار

تحت  under  |  فوق  above / over

أمام  in front of  |  خلف  behind

بين  between  |  بجانب  next to

داخل  inside  |  خارج  outside

pages.title: Dialogue — أين المكتبة؟
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — أين المكتبة؟ (Where is the library?)

ليلى  عفواً، أين المكتبة؟

رجل  المكتبة بين البنك والمدرسة.

ليلى  هل هي بجانب الحديقة؟

رجل  نعم، الحديقة أمام المكتبة مباشرةً.

ليلى  وهل هناك مقهى داخل المكتبة؟

رجل  نعم، المقهى في الطابق الأول، بجانب المدخل.

ليلى  شكراً جزيلاً!

🔍 Place prepositions used: بين (between) · بجانب (next to) · أمام (in front of) · داخل (inside) · في (in) · بجانب (next to)

pages.title: Place Prepositions — حروف الجر المكانية
pages.content:

pages.title: Quick Reference — ملخص حروف الجر

pages.title: Dialogue — رحلة إلى المدرسة
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — رحلة إلى المدرسة (Going to School)

أم  إلى أين تذهب يا أحمد؟

أحمد  أذهب إلى المدرسة، يا أمي.

أم  كيف تسافر؟ بالحافلة؟

أحمد  لا، أمشي. أمرّ عبر الحديقة وأمام المسجد.

أم  هل تصل قبل الثامنة؟

أحمد  نعم، المدرسة قريبة من البيت — فقط عشر دقائق.

titre: رسالة من لندن — A Letter from London (Arabic)

pages.title: Introduction — رسالة من لندن
pages.content:
📖 Reading & Writing — القراءة والكتابة

رسالة من لندن

A Letter from London — Read and Write in Arabic

🎯 Lesson Objectives
  • Read and understand a personal letter written in Arabic
  • Learn vocabulary related to city life and London
  • Understand Arabic letter structure (opening and closing)
  • Write your own short letter in Arabic (80–100 words)

pages.title: The Letter — رسالة من لندن
pages.content:
📖 Read the letter — اقرأ الرسالة

15 Baker Street, London · 10 October 2024

ياسمين العزيزة،

كيف حالك؟ أتمنى أن تكوني بخير. أكتب إليك من شقتي الجديدة في لندن. وصلتُ هنا منذ أسبوعين، وأنا أتأقلم ببطء مع الحياة في هذه المدينة الرائعة.

لندن مدينة كبيرة جداً وصاخبة. الشوارع مليئة بالناس من كل أنحاء العالم. أحب الحافلات الحمراء ذات الطابقين والقطار تحت الأرض — يسمّونه "الأنبوب" هنا! شقتي في وسط المدينة، قريبة من حديقة هايد بارك، وهي حديقة ضخمة وجميلة. أذهب إليها كل صباح للتنزه.

الطقس بارد وممطر، مختلف جداً عن بلدنا! لكن المدينة رائعة — هناك متاحف ومسارح وأسواق وحدائق في كل مكان. الأسبوع الماضي زرتُ المتحف البريطاني وقصر باكينغهام. كان ذلك رائعاً!

أشتاق إليك وإلى أصدقائنا كثيراً. أتمنى أن تزوريني قريباً.

مع أطيب التحيات،
صوفيا

pages.content:

OpeningClosing

pages.title: Comprehension — فهم النص
pages.content:

True or False? — صح أم خطأ؟

  1. وصلت صوفيا إلى لندن منذ شهر. (صح / خطأ)
  2. شقتها قريبة من حديقة هايد بارك. (صح / خطأ)
  3. الطقس في لندن دافئ ومشمس. (صح / خطأ)
  4. زارت المتحف البريطاني الأسبوع الماضي. (صح / خطأ)

  2. ماذا زارت الأسبوع الماضي؟

pages.title: Write Your Letter — اكتب رسالتك
pages.content:
✏️ Write Your Own Letter — اكتب رسالتك!

اكتب رسالة من 80-100 كلمة إلى صديق عن مدينة تعيش فيها أو زرتها:

  • منذ متى وأنت هناك؟
  • صِف 2-3 أشياء في المدينة
  • ماذا زرت أو فعلت؟
  • ما الذي تشتاق إليه؟
  • استخدم عبارة إغلاق مناسبة
💡 Start with: "عزيزي / عزيزتي... كيف حالك؟ أكتب إليك من..."

pages.title: Dialogue — الحديث عن الرسالة
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — الحديث عن الرسالة (Talking About the Letter)

ياسمين  وصلتني رسالة من صوفيا من لندن!

نادية  حقاً؟ كيف حالها؟ ماذا قالت؟

ياسمين  هي تتأقلم مع الحياة هناك. شقتها قريبة من حديقة هايد بارك.

نادية  رائع! هل تحب لندن؟

ياسمين  نعم! تحب الحافلات الحمراء ذات الطابقين والقطار تحت الأرض.

نادية  وكيف الطقس؟

ياسمين  بارد وممطر! هي تشتاق إلينا كثيراً. تريد أن نزورها قريباً.

نادية  يجب أن نرد عليها اليوم!

🔍 Key vocabulary: شقة (flat) · ذات الطابقين (double-decker) · تحت الأرض (underground) · تشتاق إلينا (she misses us) · نرد عليها (write back to her)

titre: السفر والمغامرة — Travel & Adventure (Arabic)

pages.title: Introduction — السفر والمغامرة
pages.content:
✈️ Reading & Speaking — القراءة والتعبير

السفر والمغامرة

Travel & Adventure — Read and Speak about Trips in Arabic

🎯 Lesson Objectives

pages.title: The Text — رحلتي الكبرى
pages.content:
📖 Read the text — اقرأ النص

رحلتي الكبرى — بقلم جيمس

بعد إنهاء دراستي الثانوية، قررتُ أن أقوم بسنة سفر وأكتشف العالم. كان ذلك أفضل قرار في حياتي!

بدأتْ رحلتي في المغرب. طرتُ من لندن إلى مراكش وقضيتُ أسبوعين أستكشف المدينة القديمة والأسواق والصحراء الجميلة. كان الناس ودودين للغاية والطعام لذيذاً جداً.

من المغرب، ركبتُ العبّارة إلى إسبانيا، ثم سافرتُ بالقطار عبر فرنسا وإيطاليا. في روما، زرتُ الكولوسيوم وأكلتُ أشهى المعكرونة. وفي باريس، صعدتُ برج إيفل عند الغروب — كان المنظر خلّاباً!

الجزء المفضل لديّ كان المغرب. الألوان، والروائح، والموسيقى... كل شيء كان ساحراً. بِتُّ في رياض تقليدي وركبتُ الجمل في صحراء الساحل!

علّمني السفر الاستقلالية والانفتاح والامتنان. التقيتُ بأشخاص من كل أنحاء العالم وتعلمتُ شيئاً جديداً كل يوم. أوصي بهذه التجربة للجميع!

pages.content:

Answer the questions — أجب على الأسئلة

  2. كيف سافر من المغرب إلى إسبانيا؟
  3. ما هو الجزء المفضل لديه في الرحلة؟ ولماذا؟

  2. وجهته المفضلة كانت إيطاليا. (صح / خطأ)
  3. بات في رياض تقليدي. (صح / خطأ)

pages.title: Write Your Trip — اكتب عن رحلتك
pages.content:
✏️ Write About Your Trip — اكتب عن رحلتك!

اكتب فقرة من 80-100 كلمة عن رحلة حقيقية أو خيالية:

  • إلى أين ذهبت؟
  • كيف سافرت؟
  • ماذا رأيت وفعلت؟
  • ما الذي أثّر فيك أكثر؟
  • هل توصي بهذه الوجهة؟
💡 Use the past tense: ذهبتُ، رأيتُ، أكلتُ، زرتُ، ركبتُ...
Example: "في الصيف الماضي، ذهبتُ إلى... زرتُ... كان الطعام..."

pages.title: Dialogue — الحديث عن الرحلة
pages.content:
💬 Dialogue — الحديث عن الرحلة (Talking About the Trip)

جيمس  هل استمتعتَ بعطلتك؟

سارة  كانت رائعة! ذهبتُ إلى المغرب لمدة أسبوعين.

جيمس  حقاً؟ كيف سافرتِ إلى هناك؟

سارة  طرتُ إلى مراكش — المناظر كانت خلّابة!

جيمس  ماذا زرتِ؟

سارة  استكشفتُ المدينة القديمة وبِتُّ في رياض تقليدي. كان ساحراً!

جيمس  هل توصين به؟

سارة  بالتأكيد! أنا ممتنّة لهذه التجربة الرائعة!

🔍 Past tense verbs used: ذهبتُ (went) · سافرتِ (travelled) · طرتُ (flew) · زرتِ (visited) · استكشفتُ (explored) · بِتُّ (stayed)
